county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1804 from Somerset, Bedford, and Huntingdon counties. Named from the Latin for Wales. Johnstown is the major city, infamous for the 1889 Johnstown Flood that killed 2,209 people. Major bituminous coal mining and steel industry center. Massive immigration from Wales, Ireland, and Eastern Europe. Cambria Iron Works (later Bethlehem Steel) was a major employer.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cambria County research must account for the devastating 1889 Johnstown Flood which destroyed many personal and church records though county courthouse records in Ebensburg survived. The Johnstown Area Heritage Association (JAHA) preserves flood-related records and industrial heritage materials. The Cambria County Historical Society maintains genealogical collections. Recorder of Deeds in Ebensburg has records from 1804. Coal company records document immigrant miners from Wales, Ireland, Poland, Slovakia, Italy, and other nations. Check Cambria County Library System for local history. Catholic parish records are essential for Eastern European immigrants. The Welsh settlement at Ebensburg and Beulah is well documented in Welsh-American heritage sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1804; LANDEX online system

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1804. LANDEX online search system.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1804. Courthouse in Ebensburg survived 1889 flood.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1804.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Cambria County Marriages
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1810. Coal miner and steel worker occupations documented.
Welsh Congregational, Irish and Eastern European Catholic, Lutheran, and Methodist records. Critical for immigrant genealogy.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Variable. Some flood damage to Johnstown parish records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Johnstown Tribune, Democrat. 1889 flood coverage and aftermath. Coal and steel industry news.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1860 from Clinton, Elk, McKean, and Potter counties. Smallest PA county by population. Heavily forested with timber and tanning industries. Named for US Senator Simon Cameron. Sinnemahoning Creek valley. Remote mountainous terrain.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cameron County is PA's least populous county, making genealogical research more focused. The Cameron County Historical Society maintains local collections. Recorder of Deeds in Emporium has records from 1860. For pre-1860 research, check Clinton, Elk, McKean, and Potter counties based on township location. Lumber company records document timber workers and their families. The tanning industry also generated employment records. Most families are of Scotch-Irish, German, or English descent. Methodist and Presbyterian church records are primary religious sources. The remoteness of the county means many families are documented primarily through census, tax, and deed records rather than church records.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of the Counties of McKean, Elk, Cameron and Potter, Pennsylvania (1890)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.

Emporium newspapers with local notices and legal advertisements.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1843 from Northampton and Monroe counties. Named for its anthracite coal deposits. Originally called Mauch Chunk (county seat renamed Jim Thorpe in 1954). Major anthracite mining county. The Molly Maguires labor conflict (1870s) centered here. Lehigh Coal and Navigation Company dominated the economy. Irish and Welsh miners predominated.
county_metadata.research_tips: Carbon County research is closely tied to anthracite coal mining heritage. The Carbon County Historical Society (Mauch Chunk Museum) has mining records, family files, and Molly Maguire trial transcripts. Recorder of Deeds in Jim Thorpe holds records from 1843. For pre-1843 families check Northampton and Monroe counties. Irish Catholic parish records are essential for mining families. Welsh Congregational and Methodist records document the Welsh mining community. The Lehigh Coal and Navigation Company records at Hagley Museum and Library document employees. Asa Packer (Lehigh Valley Railroad founder) papers at Lehigh University. The 'Old Jail Museum' has Molly Maguire execution records. Check NARA Philadelphia for naturalization records of immigrant miners.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation. Mining land leases significant.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1843.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Carbon County Marriages

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Irish Catholic parishes (Immaculate Conception, St. Joseph), Welsh Congregational and Methodist chapels. Mining community records.
Mauch Chunk newspapers with mining news, labor disputes, and vital records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good coverage. Molly Maguire era papers valuable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Mifflin, Northumberland, Lycoming, and Huntingdon counties. Named for its central location in PA. Home to Penn State University (founded 1855 as Farmers High School). Iron furnaces in 19th century. Mix of agricultural valleys and forested mountains. Penns Valley and Nittany Valley settlement patterns.
county_metadata.research_tips: Centre County Historical Society in Bellefonte maintains genealogical files and local history collections. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1800. For pre-1800 families, check the four parent counties based on township location. Penn State University Special Collections has local history manuscripts and agricultural records. Iron furnace records document workers and their families. German settlers dominated the valleys; Scotch-Irish settlers the ridges. Lutheran, Reformed, and Presbyterian church records are primary sources. The Bellefonte courthouse has well-preserved records. Centre County Library has genealogical materials. Penns Valley area has strong Mennonite and Amish heritage documented in community records.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Centre and Clinton Counties, Pennsylvania (1883)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1800; LANDEX online system

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of three original counties created by William Penn in 1682. Seat moved from Chester to West Chester in 1786 when Delaware County was carved off. Quaker heartland from earliest settlement. Welsh Tract settlement from 1680s. Agricultural county becoming suburban. Brandywine Valley historically significant. Longwood Gardens area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Chester County is arguably the most genealogically important PA county outside Philadelphia due to its Quaker and Welsh heritage. The Chester County Historical Society in West Chester has an extraordinary collection including deed abstracts from 1681, family files, and the complete run of Chester County court records. Friends Historical Library at Swarthmore holds Chester Monthly Meeting records from 1681 with births, marriages, deaths, disownments, and removals. Recorder of Deeds has colonial records from 1688. The Welsh Tract families (1682-1700s) are documented in Browning's Welsh Settlement of Pennsylvania. Brandywine Valley research involves du Pont family papers at Hagley Museum. Chester County Archives and Records Services is a professional archival facility. For pre-1789 Delaware County families, check Chester County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Colonial deed records from 1688; online index system. One of PA's oldest deed collections

source_profiles.repository: Chester County Government Services Center, West Chester
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial-era deed records from 1688. Penn proprietary land system. Online indexes.
Wills, administrations, inventories from 1681. Among oldest probate records in PA.
Chester, Concord, Birmingham, Bradford, Goshen meetings. The richest Quaker records in PA covering births, marriages, deaths, disownments, certificates of removal.

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Chester County Marriages

Original warrants, surveys, patents. Welsh Tract grants particularly important.
West Chester newspapers with legal notices, estate sales, and Quaker community news.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1839 from Venango and Armstrong counties. Clarion River runs through the county. Timber, iron, and later oil industries. Clarion University (founded 1867). Rural county with German and Scotch-Irish settlers. Glass manufacturing in some areas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clarion County Historical Society maintains genealogical files and cemetery records. Recorder of Deeds in Clarion has records from 1839. For pre-1839 families check Venango and Armstrong counties. Iron furnace records document early industrial workers. Timber industry records are at the historical society. German Lutheran and Scotch-Irish Presbyterian church records are primary sources. Clarion University Special Collections has local history. The Redbank Valley area connects to Armstrong County family networks. Check FamilySearch for microfilmed tax and church records.

Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1839.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1804 from Huntingdon and Lycoming counties. Named for clearings made by bison. Major timber and coal county. Bituminous coal mining significant from mid-1800s. West Branch Susquehanna River lumber rafting era. DuBois industrial center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clearfield County Historical Society maintains family files and local records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1804. For pre-1804 families check Huntingdon and Lycoming counties. Timber industry records document lumber rafters and sawmill workers along the West Branch Susquehanna. Coal mining records document later immigrant workers. The DuBois area has strong industrial heritage. Lutheran, Presbyterian, and Methodist church records are primary sources. The Clearfield County seat town itself has well-preserved courthouse records. Shaw Memorial Library in Clearfield has local history. Curwensville area records connect to early settlement patterns.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations, estate inventories from 1804.

Clearfield and DuBois newspapers with timber era news and vital records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1839 from Lycoming and Centre counties. West Branch Susquehanna River runs through county. Major lumber rafting center in 19th century. Lock Haven was a boom town during timber era. Logging and lumber mills dominated economy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clinton County Historical Society in Lock Haven maintains genealogical files and lumber era records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1839. For pre-1839 families check Lycoming and Centre counties. Lumber company records document the rafting era when millions of board feet floated down the West Branch. Lock Haven University (formerly State Normal School) archives have local history. Presbyterian, Methodist, and Lutheran church records serve as primary sources. The Annie Halenbake Ross Library has local history and genealogical materials. Renovo area has railroad heritage records.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Centre and Clinton Counties, Pennsylvania (1883)

source_profiles.source_name: PA State Archives — Clinton County Marriages

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1813 from Northumberland County. Named for Columbus. Bloomsburg is the only incorporated town in PA. Agricultural with anthracite coal mining in eastern portion. Catawissa and Centralia areas. Bloomsburg University (founded 1839).
county_metadata.research_tips: Columbia County Historical and Genealogical Society in Bloomsburg is the primary resource with extensive local collections. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1813. For pre-1813 families check Northumberland County. The anthracite coal mining region in eastern Columbia connects to the Schuylkill mining heritage. Centralia, the famous underground mine fire town, has records at the historical society. German and Scotch-Irish heritage predominates. Lutheran, Reformed, and Presbyterian church records are primary. Bloomsburg University archives have local history. The Fishing Creek valley has early settlement records.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Columbia and Montour Counties, Pennsylvania (1887)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny County. Northwestern PA. French Creek runs through county. Meadville is home to Allegheny College (1815). Oil industry significant after Drake Well (1859) in adjacent Venango County. Titusville oil boom affected northern Crawford. Agriculture and manufacturing.
county_metadata.research_tips: Crawford County Historical Society in Meadville has genealogical files and local records including oil industry materials. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1800. For pre-1800 check Allegheny County. The oil boom (1860s) brought workers documented in oil company records and Titusville-area documents. Allegheny College archives have local manuscripts. New England settlers (Connecticut Western Reserve influence from nearby Ohio) brought Congregational and Baptist traditions. Presbyterian and Methodist church records are important. The French Creek valley has early settlement documentation. Baldwin-Reynolds House Museum has manuscript collections. Meadville Theological School records document Unitarian families.
